data.title: Has the Identity of Bitcoin's Creator Finally Been Uncovered?
data.description:
For years, the identity of Bitcoin's creator, Satoshi Nakamoto, has remained one of the biggest mysteries in finance and technology. However, a new 10,000-word investigation by The New York Times suggests that British computer scientist Adam Back could be the person behind the alias. Beyond simple curiosity, the answer matters because Satoshi is believed to hold about 1.1 million Bitcoin, making him one of the largest holders in the world.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

It is worth noting that Back's role in crypto history makes the theory more compelling. Indeed, in 1997, he created Hashcash, a system that introduced the idea of proof of work, which later became the foundation of Bitcoin. In fact, Satoshi directly contacted Back before releasing the Bitcoin white paper to reference his work. According to the investigation, researchers compared Satoshi's known writings with Back's and found strong similarities in writing style, including grammar patterns and formatting habits. These similarities, along with historical connections between the two, are a key part of the case.

Still, Back has firmly denied that he is Satoshi. He stated publicly that while he was involved early in cryptography and digital cash research, he is not the creator of Bitcoin. Despite this, the investigation argues that the number of similarities is difficult to ignore. Importantly, the issue also has real financial implications. Back is currently the CEO of a company that is preparing to go public, and if he were Satoshi, his massive Bitcoin holdings could be considered important information for investors.

Is Bitcoin a Good Buy?

Using TipRanks' technical analysis tool, the indicators seem to point to a positive outlook for Bitcoin. Indeed, the summary section pictured below shows that 10 indicators are Bullish, compared to five Neutral and seven Bearish indicators.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Strategy Stock (MSTR) Set for Over 100% Jump? Why BTIG Analyst Says 'Buy' Now
data.description:
Shares of Strategy (MSTR) trended higher on Wednesday, supported by rising Bitcoin prices. Adding to the momentum, BTIG analyst Andrew Harte reiterated a Buy rating and a $250 price target, pointing to strong demand for the company's new STRC offering. The price target implies about 102% upside from current levels.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

For context, "STRC" is a high-yield preferred stock issued by Strategy that pays a variable dividend and is used to raise capital to buy more Bitcoin without diluting common shareholders.

Why This Top Analyst Is Bullish on Strategy

Harte sees STRC as a key step in Strategy's plan to grow its Bitcoin holdings and change how investors gain exposure to the asset. It pays about an 11.5% variable dividend and helps the company raise capital to buy more Bitcoin. It's a way to make Bitcoin exposure less risky for some investors while boosting upside for MSTR stockholders.

The analyst explained that this structure allows Strategy to pass on a fixed portion of Bitcoin returns to STRC investors, while any gains above that level go to common shareholders. This effectively "strips out" part of Bitcoin's volatility and turns it into a more stable, income-like product.

He also noted that demand is already strong, with Strategy issuing more than $1.5 billion of STRC in March alone, showing that the model is gaining traction among investors.

The analyst believes this approach could allow Strategy stock to outperform Bitcoin itself over time. As STRC scales and becomes more liquid, it could attract more investors and highlight its appeal as an alternative to traditional fixed income, supporting further upside in MSTR shares.

Is Strategy a Buy, Sell, or Hold? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSTR stock based on 12 unanimous Buys assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average MSTR price target of $284.17 per share implies 129.69% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Rally on U.S.-Iran Truce as Tehran Calls Out Peace Proposal Violations
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed higher on Wednesday after the U.S. and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire and a temporary reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for QQQ bulls

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • Following the ceasefire, traffic through Hormuz remains limited, with four vessels safely crossing through. That's well below the pre-war daily average of 135 ships.
  • Iran threatened to keep the passageway closed after Israel struck several sites in Beirut.
  • Officials from the U.S. and Iran are set to meet over the weekend in Islamabad for peace negotiations.
  • President Trump announced a 50% tariff on any country that supplies Iran with weapons.
  • Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf accused the U.S. of violating three terms within Iran's 10-point proposal. Ghalibaf pointed to Israel striking Lebanon, the entry of a drone into Iranian airspace, and restrictions on uranium enrichment.
  • The odds of a 25 bps rate cut by year-end nearly doubled to 25.2% from 13.6% as oil futures plummeted.
  • Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD (AMD) closed higher after Bank of America (BAC) raised its 2026 semiconductor revenue projection to $1.3 trillion from $1 trillion.
  • Apple (AAPL) is a new favorite among retail investors and saw $65.3 million of net inflows on Tuesday.
  • Tesla (TSLA) closed lower despite rolling out Full Self-Driving (FSD) version 14.3. Last year, CEO Elon Musk said the update would make vehicles "feel like it is sentient."

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Industrials was the best-performing sector on Wednesday, trailed closely by communication services. Brent crude oil futures (BZ) dropped by 12% following the U.S.-Iran ceasefire, lowering fuel costs and potentially boosting profit margins for manufacturing, transportation, and logistics companies.

Several industrial stocks led the industrial sector's gains, including:

  • United Airlines (UAL)
  • Vertiv Holdings (VRT)
  • Comfort Systems USA (FIX)
  • Cummins (CMI)
  • GE Aerospace (GE)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the energy sector faced the largest drawdown. While industrial companies benefit from lower oil prices, energy companies struggle. With cheaper oil, producers and refiners are likely to see thinner profit margins, putting pressure on earnings. At the same time, energy is still the top-performing sector this year with a 34% return.

Notable energy stocks trading lower include:

  • APA (APA)
  • Marathon Petroleum (MPC)
  • Exxon Mobil (XOM)
  • Occidental Petroleum (OXY)
  • ConocoPhillips (COP)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Netflix Sets its Sights on Argentina, Netflix Stock (NASDAQ:NFLX) Notches Up
data.description:
While streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) is available throughout large portions of the world, it seems to be putting a particular focus on Argentina lately. It has set up several new overtures and operations in the country recently, which has some wondering why. Investors are taking a wait-and-see approach, based on the fractional bump in Netflix shares seen in the closing minutes of Wednesday's trading.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

One of the biggest moves that Netflix made in Argentina was opening up a new office in Buenos Aires, the capital. It joins offices Netflix recently opened in Brazil and Mexico of late, which were at the time considered production centers in Latin America. Thus, the Argentine location is likely considered a production center as well. Netflix is also stepping up its productions in the region, with plans for four new shows. A second season of The Eternaut is in line, with hopes to release in 2027. A drama series and multiple documentaries are also set to arrive eventually.

Netflix's vice president of content in Latin America, Francisco Ramos, noted, "We are signaling that we are here to stay because all of these have one thing in common, they have been tailor-made for us. They have the DNA of our offices, like the ones in London or L.A. When you walk through the door you feel you're in a Netflix office."

"Where All the Bodies are Buried"

Before his death in 2025, Terry Bollea—more commonly known as Hulk Hogan—was perhaps the biggest name in wrestling for the last 40 years. When you have been around as long as he has, you learn a few things about how productions work and, as he put it, "…where all the bodies are buried." A new documentary, Hulk Hogan: Real American, offers some insight into just what the Hulkster knew and when he knew it.

You will likely have to watch the documentary yourself to find out just how much he knew, but reports suggest the documentary will feature not only his wrestling career, but also, home videos with himself and his children Nick and Brooke. Reports note the documentary will premiere April 22.

Is Netflix Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on NFLX stock based on 31 Buys and 10 Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 4.52% rally in its share price over the past year, the average NFLX price target of $114.61 per share implies 15.24% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: 3 Defense ETFs to Watch as U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Starts to Unravel
data.description:
Iran's parliamentary speaker, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, accused the U.S. of breaking the newly announced two-week ceasefire. Notably, he pointed to a long history of distrust by arguing that the U.S. has repeatedly failed to honor its commitments and that the same pattern is happening again. As a result, defense ETFs are back in focus, as investors may look for exposure through:

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

  • Invesco Aerospace & Defense ETF (PPA)
  • iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF (ITA)
  • SPDR S&P Aerospace & Defense ETF (XAR)

Ghalibaf also claimed that several parts of Iran's ceasefire proposal have already been violated. He specifically mentioned continued Israeli attacks in Lebanon, as well as disagreements over Iran's right to enrich uranium. Because of these issues, he said that further negotiations or a bilateral ceasefire no longer make sense. So far, the White House has not responded publicly to these claims, which adds more uncertainty around whether the agreement will hold.

There is also a growing disagreement between the U.S. and Iran over what the ceasefire actually includes, especially when it comes to the Strait of Hormuz. President Donald Trump has pushed for the Strait to reopen fully and without restrictions. However, Iran is reportedly considering charging tolls for ships to pass through.

Which Defense ETF Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the three ETFs mentioned above, analysts think that XAR has the most room to run. In fact, XAR's price target of $336.03 per share implies 24% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: It's Time to Buy the Magnificent 7 Stocks Again, Says Goldman Sachs
data.description:
Wall Street investment bank Goldman Sachs (GS) says it's time to reconsider the beaten down mega-cap technology stocks collectively known as the Magnificent 7.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for NVDA bears

With markets rebounding on news of a ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran, Goldman Sachs is urging investors to look for growth opportunities in the Magnificent 7 names that include Apple (AAPL), Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOGL), Meta Platforms (META), Microsoft (MSFT), Nvidia (NVDA), and Tesla (TSLA).

In fact, Peter Oppenheimer, Chief Global Equity Strategist at Goldman Sachs, says that a "generational buying opportunity" has opened up in U.S. technology stocks after they got knocked lower in recent months over concerns about and artificial intelligence (AI) bubble and capital expenditures.

Growth Opportunity

In fact, Oppenheimer points out in a note to clients that technology stocks are in the midst of their worst stretch of performance against the broader market in 50 years. This has opened up the best opportunity in decades to buy the beaten-down technology stocks.

The Goldman Sachs executive adds that mega-cap technology stocks such as Microsoft and Nvidia have been overly punished and that the declines don't reflect the continued strong growth rates of these companies. He points out that many leading technology stocks, such as Apple and Amazon are now trading at lower multiples than many consumer stocks.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Hedge Funds Are Covering Their Shorts at Fastest Pace Since March 2020 Crash
data.description:
Hedge funds are quickly closing out their bets against U.S. stocks at the fastest pace since the market crash in March 2020, according to Goldman Sachs (GS). This comes after President Donald Trump announced a two-week ceasefire in the conflict with Iran. As a result, many hedge funds rushed to cover their short positions, especially those tied to major indexes and ETFs. According to Goldman's John Flood, investors are now starting to believe that this could be the beginning of the end of the conflict.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

At the same time, this wave of short covering is helping push the market higher. Major indexes like the S&P 500 (SPY), Nasdaq (QQQ), and Dow (DIA) have all risen by more than 2% as investors reposition. Prior to this move, hedge funds had built up large short positions, with exposure reaching about 12% of total portfolios, the highest level since the pandemic. As a result, the sudden change is creating what Goldman describes as a "squeeze phase," where rising prices force more investors to exit bearish bets.

Looking ahead, this change in positioning could lead to a rotation in the market. Sectors that performed well during the conflict, such as energy (XLE) and defense (ITA), may see short-term pullbacks as investors take profits. Meanwhile, capital could start flowing back into areas that were strong before the conflict, particularly semiconductor and memory-related stocks.

Is SPY Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on SPY stock based on 413 Buys, 83 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average SPY price target of $828.04 per share implies 23% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Rate Cut Odds Surge as Oil Slide Tempers Inflation Fears
data.description:
Brent crude oil futures (BZ) plummeted 13% on Wednesday, registering the largest drop since 2020, after the U.S. and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Rate-cut odds have fallen significantly since the start of the conflict on February 28, but are now moving higher as fears of oil-driven inflation ease. While the Fed is widely expected to hold rates steady at the April 29 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting, traders now assign higher odds of a rate cut at later meetings.

Rate Cut Odds Rise as Fed Highlights Risks to Dual Mandate

The odds of one 25 bps rate cut by year-end have nearly doubled from the prior day, rising to 25.2% from 13.6% on the CME FedWatch tool. However, they remain below levels seen a month ago when they stood at 32%. The odds of rates staying steady remain the most likely outcome with 71.6% odds, down from 80.9% a day ago.

Meanwhile, minutes from the Fed's latest meeting showed that officials are concerned about both inflation and employment, with the war further complicating the interest rate path. Policymakers still project one rate cut in 2026, unchanged from their outlook in December.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: KRE and IAT Regional Bank ETFs Rise Ahead of Next FED Meeting. Should You Buy Now?
data.description:
Regional Bank ETFs are in focus as U.S. markets reassess growth prospects ahead of the Federal Reserve's next FOMC meeting scheduled for April 28-29. The update is important for funds such as the SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF (KRE) and the iShares U.S. Regional Banks ETF (IAT), as it provides signals about inflation trends and the Fed's interest-rate stance. 

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Notably, KRE and IAT are seeing a sudden price resurgence, prompting investors to assess whether current market conditions present a favorable entry point ahead of the FED policy meeting.

Regional Bank ETFs Rise After U.S.-Iran Ceasefire

Regional bank ETFs have seen a significant decline in recent months. The KRE and IAT ETFs have both plummeted by roughly 7-10% from their February highs. However, recent market data shows that both ETFs are showing signs of a recovery ahead of the upcoming FED decision. KRE is currently up by more than 2.5%, while IAT has jumped nearly 3%. This resurgence is likely due to the news of a ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran.

Although IAT and KRE prices are rising, they are still down more than 7% year to date. For some investors, this decline could be seen as a buying opportunity, especially as prices show strength ahead of the upcoming FED meeting. The previous downtrend in both ETFs came as regional banks remained sensitive to changes in interest rates and escalating geopolitical tensions.

Unlike larger banks, these institutions rely more heavily on lending activity, increasing earnings exposure to borrowing costs, credit risks, and shifts in economic growth expectations. Additionally, the spike in oil prices linked to the U.S.-Iran conflict has added to inflation concerns, weighing on lending demand and contributing to weaker performance in banking stocks.

Why Regional Bank ETFs Could Benefit from a Rate Cut

The Federal Reserve maintained the federal funds rate at 3.50%–3.75% at its most recent meeting, signaling a continued pause in policy direction. But if geopolitical tensions ease, oil prices and yields could decline, improving overall market sentiment. This could also reduce uncertainty and potentially increase expectations that the FED may consider rate cuts later in the year.

Such a shift may be enough to push regional bank ETFs upward, as a lower-rate environment can help ease funding pressures, support loan growth, and improve profit margins. If this occurs, the current pullback in ETFs such as KRE and IAT could set up stronger returns for investors who bought before the FOMC meeting.

Is it Good to Invest in Bank ETFs?

Regional bank ETFs offer diversified exposure to many local bank stocks, reducing the risk of relying on a single institution. For investors looking to buy shares of regional banks, KRE currently carries a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from Wall Street analysts tracked on TipRanks' ETF Center. The ETF has an average price target of about $77 and an upside potential of over 13%. Similarly, IAT is rated a "Moderate Buy," but has an average price target of about $64, representing a more than 12% upside potential.
data.author: Joy Iyke
